title: CRITICAL: Leverage invariant base case unproven for arbitrary initial vertex

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Gap Description

The proof (Step 1) says 'Pick any v0 in V' but never verifies the leverage invariant l(S0) <= 4|S0| = 4 for the initial singleton S0 = {v0}.

## Counterexample

For the star K_{1,n-1} with unit weights and center vertex c: by Foster's identity, all n-1 edges have equal leverage summing to n-1, so each has leverage 1. Thus l({c}, V\{c}) = n-1 >> 4 for n > 5.

Since the Greedy Lemma takes l(S) <= 4|S| as an explicit hypothesis, the entire induction collapses without this base case.

## Why It Matters

The Greedy Lemma is applied sigma times. At step 0 we need l(S0) <= 4. Without establishing this, we cannot execute the first greedy step.

## Fix

Apply Claim 3 to T = V: sum_{v in V} l(v,V) <= 2(n-1) < 2n, so the average is < 2. By Markov with threshold 4, more than half of all vertices satisfy l(v0, V\{v0}) <= 4.

Change initialization to: 'Pick v0 in V with l(v0, V) <= 4 (exists by Claim 3 + Markov applied to T=V).'

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Analysis and Fix

### Gap Confirmed

The critic is correct. The original Step 1 said "Pick any v0 in V", but never established the leverage invariant l(S0) <= 4|S0| = 4 for the initial singleton S0 = {v0}. The Greedy Lemma requires l(S) <= 4|S| as an explicit hypothesis, so without this base case the induction collapses.

The counterexample is valid: for K_{1,n-1} with unit weights, the center vertex c has l({c}, V\{c}) = n-1 (all n-1 edges with leverage 1 are incident to c), which exceeds 4 for n > 5.

### Fix Applied

Step 1 now selects v0 using a Markov argument:

1. Foster identity: sum_{(s,t) in E} l(s,t) <= n-1.
2. Double-counting: sum_{v in V} l({v}, V\{v}) = 2 * sum_{edges} l(e) <= 2(n-1) < 2n.
3. Markov with threshold 4: at most 2(n-1)/4 < n/2 vertices have l({v}) > 4. So more than half satisfy l({v}) <= 4.
4. Initialization: Pick any such v0. Then l(S0) = l({v0}, V\{v0}) <= 4 = 4|S0| establishes the leverage invariant at step 0.

The barrier invariant B^{u0}_sigma(S0) <= phi is unchanged (holds for any v0 since G_{S0} has no edges).

With both invariants verified at step 0, the Greedy Lemma applies inductively for all sigma steps, and the proof is complete.

### Verification

For K_{1,n-1}: each of the n-1 leaf vertices has l({v}) = 1 <= 4, so the Markov selection picks any leaf, correctly avoiding the high-leverage center vertex.
